title: morella punctata
intro: Morella punctata, also known as the prickly ash or toothache tree, is a plant species native to North America. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Morella punctata include relieving toothache pain, reducing swelling and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. Morella punctata is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be used to make a topical paste for external applications.
